title: eugenia ugni
intro: Eugenia ugni, also known as the Chilean guava or strawberry myrtle, is a small evergreen shrub native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of ascorbic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Eugenia ugni include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds, fever, and digestive issues. Eugenia ugni can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products, taking advantage of its sweet and slightly tart flavor.
